board_csa_architect.notes: Two-table design is clean: orgs.data_sharing_opt_in for privacy, marketplace_submissions for approval queue. Trigger-based enforcement of Enterprise immutability is the right pattern — mirrors REQ-L6-33 tier constraints. agent_templates column additions are nullable and backward-compatible.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: Privacy boundaries are strong. Enterprise immutability via trigger is tamper-resistant. RLS on submissions prevents cross-tenant visibility. Published templates use sanitized copy — no system prompt leakage to marketplace. data_sharing_opt_in is metadata-only — no raw data exposure.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: No AI/VRAM impact. This is database schema + API + UI work. Approval queue is a simple CRUD pattern. Template publishing reuses existing template.registry.ts INSERT path.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: XL effort is appropriate for dual-concept scope. Phased rollout recommended: privacy tiers first (schema only), then marketplace submission flow. No infra changes needed — existing DB + containers.

board_red_team.notes: Conditions: (1) Sanitization gate: when publishing tenant agent to marketplace, strip system_prompt of any org-specific references, API keys, or internal URLs before copying to agent_templates. (2) Rate-limit submissions to 5/day per org to prevent approval queue flooding. (3) Audit log entry on every approval/rejection for accountability. (4) rev_share_percentage must be immutable post-publish — add CHECK constraint or audit trigger. Failure scenario: tenant embeds malicious instructions in agent that bypass sandbox when forked by other tenants.
